comment: Restore-then-replay bit-identity (DESIGN_QUESTS §10, the determinism ask): a hook-bearing quest whose `custom` predicate fires INSIDE advance_hours catch-up (elapsed-days gate, watched on time.day) and whose on_resolve rolls the sanctioned q.roll dice. replay_advance runs the same advance TWICE from a restored snapshot (clock rewound too) and asserts the journal.*/choice.*/test.* namespaces are byte-identical — the playtest desk's restore-then-replay produces identical latches, and q.roll's dice are stable per day. The gap assert proves the memoir stamps the REPLAYED day (catch-up honesty), not the wall day it was noticed.

stages.journal: Nothing to do until two days pass.
Two days passed, and the wait was over.
